channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: China begins repatriation from Thailand of more than 1,000 online scam workers rescued from Myanmar
item.description: An airlift carrying more than 1,000 Chinese nationals who had worked at online scam centers in eastern Myanmar began Thursday, after the rescued workers were taken across the border to Thailand and put on chartered flights to China.
item.dc:creator: Jutarat Skulpichetrat And Grant Peck, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel grieves as Hamas turns over the remains said to be of a mother and her 2 young children
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Ariel and Kfir Bibas — two of the youngest hostages held in Gaza — became a symbol for Israelis of the brutality of Hamas' Oct. 7, 2023, attack.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany votes Sunday for a new government that will shape Europe's response to an assertive Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German voters go to the polls Sunday to elect a new parliament that will determine how the country is run for the next four years.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kim Sae-ron's death underscores the huge pressure on South Korean celebrities
item.description: Actor Kim Sae-ron’s death adds to the list of celebrity deaths in South Korea that underscore the enormous pressure they face under the gaze of a relentlessly unforgiving media that pounces on their every misstep.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas returns bodies of 4 Israeli hostages said to include a mother and her 2 young children
item.description: Hamas has handed over the bodies of four Israeli hostages, said to include a mother and her two children who have long been feared dead and had come to embody the nation’s agony following the Oct. 7, 2023, attack.
item.dc:creator: Mohammad Jahjouh, Wafaa Shurafa And Melanie Lidman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As the Ukraine war reaches its 3-year mark, Russia seems to have time on its side as talks start
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin's invasion of Ukraine three years ago has turned into Europe’s largest conflict since World War II.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's freeze on foreign aid could give China an opening on the world stage
item.description: President Donald Trump’s restrictions on foreign aid and targeting of a key agency funding programs around the world may be offering an opening to China.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rep. Don Bacon says Trump 'wrong' to call Zelensky a dictator
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.) said President Trump was “wrong” to call Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky a dictator on Wednesday and for appearing to be “standing on the side” of Russian President Vladimir Putin. “The president needs a do-over day – and start again.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Yoon appears in 2 different South Korean courts while defending his martial law decree
item.description: Driven across Seoul in prison transport vehicles, South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol appeared in two different courts, contesting his arrest on rebellion charges in one and pleading to preserve his presidency in the other, both related to his brief imposition of martial law in December.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Italy PM Meloni visits pope in hospital, says he's in good spirits as tests show slight improvement
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis for 20 minutes in the hospital Wednesday and reported he was “alert and responsive” and full of good humor, despite his diagnosis of pneumonia and a complicated bronchial infection that has sidelined the 88-year-old pontiff for six days
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, ANDREA ROSA and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US declares Mexican cartels terrorist organizations, Musk floats drone strikes
item.description: The State Department has declared several Mexican drug cartels as terrorist organizations, with Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) leader Elon Musk floating the idea of drone strikes against the groups.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Pence denounces Trump's claim Ukraine started war
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence denounced his ex-boss, President Trump, over his recent claim that Ukraine started the war with Russia, a conflict that has been ongoing for three years. “Mr. President, Ukraine did not 'start' this war.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 8 in 10 say Putin should not be trusted: Survey
item.description: The Quinnipiac University Poll released Wednesday found 81% of American voters believe Putin should not be trusted, while Trump's administration attempts to broker a peace deal to end the conflict between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Pakistan steps up arrests of Afghans without papers. Kabul says it's a move to expel all refugees
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Authorities have stepped up arrests of Afghan citizens in Pakistan's capital and a nearby city in an effort that the Afghan Embassy in Islamabad described on Wednesday as a push to force the expulsion of all Afghan refugees from the country.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement, receives visit from Italian PM Giorgia Meloni
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis in the hospital on Wednesday where she spent 20 minutes with the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: "We found that more than 5 billion people—ie, more than 60% of the world's population—do not have access to safe, quality, and affordable medical oxygen services," the commission wrote.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: US condemns 'dangerous' Chinese maneuvers after near miss with Philippine patrol plane
item.description: The U.S. condemned what it called “dangerous” Chinese maneuvers over the South China Sea after a close encounter between a Chinese helicopter and a Philippine patrol plane on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Italian Prime Minister Meloni visits Pope Francis in hospital, says he's in good spirits
item.description: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis in hospital on Wednesday and reported he was full of good humor following his diagnosis of pneumonia and a bronchial infection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hawley calls for new inspector general to investigate Ukraine spending
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) pledged to introduce legislation to track U.S. dollars designated for Ukraine in its war against Russia, after citing the possibility of corrupt spending under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump crafted 'win-win' situation for US, Ukraine economic plan: Bessent
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said during an interview that President Trump crafted a “win-win” situation for the potential U.S.-Ukraine economic plan, a proposal that, in his view, will boost Kyiv’s security and bring the two countries into a closer relationship.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Philippine village battles dengue by offering bounties for mosquitos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A village in the densely populated Philippine capital region launched a battle against dengue Wednesday by offering a token bounty to residents for captured mosquitos — dead or alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who attacked Japan's ex-Prime Minister Kishida is convicted and sentenced to 10 years in prison
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A Japanese court on Wednesday convicted a man who threw a homemade pipe bomb at Japan's former Prime Minister Fumio Kishida at a 2023 campaign event, sentencing him to 10 years in prison, court officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s former President Bolsonaro charged over alleged coup that included a plan to poison Lula
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Brazil's prosecutor-general on Tuesday formally charged former President Jair Bolsonaro with attempting a coup to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, in a plot that included a plan to poison his successor and current President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and kill a Sup
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES and MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeepSeek accused of sharing user data with TikTok owner ByteDance
item.description: "We confirmed DeepSeek communicating with ByteDance," a South Korean data protection regulator told Yonhap News Agency.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it will free 6 living hostages and hand over 4 bodies, accelerating Gaza releases
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A top Hamas official says the militant group will free six living Israeli hostages on Saturday and return the bodies of four others on Thursday, a surprise acceleration in releases apparently in trade for Israel’s allowing mobile homes and construction equipment into the devastated Gaza
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: A Chinese navy helicopter flew within 10 feet of a Philippine patrol plane over the disputed Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea; the plane did not have to alter its path to avoid a collision.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians should be able to leave Gaza if they choose: Israel envoy
item.description: Hamas is speeding up the hostage exchange with Israel, announcing six people will be released Saturday instead of three.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis has bilateral pneumonia but remains in good spirits, the Vatican says
item.description: Nevertheless the pope is in good spirits and is grateful for the prayers for his recovery, Vatican spokesman Matteo Bruni said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former USAID official warns China is already looking to fill void left by paused programs
item.description: China is reportedly trying to fill the voids left in several countries by the Trump administration's freeze of USAID funding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN human rights chief accuses Rwanda-backed rebels in Congo of killing, recruiting children
item.description: The U.N. human rights chief accused Rwanda-backed rebels in Congo of killing children and attacking hospitals and warehouses storing humanitarian aid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: OVER THE SCARBOROUGH SHOAL, South China Sea (AP) — A Chinese navy helicopter flew within 10 feet (3 meters) of a Philippine patrol plane on Tuesday in a disputed area of the South China Sea, prompting the Filipino pilot to warn by radio: “You are flying too close, you are very dangerous.
item.dc:creator: JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US citizen detained on drug smuggling charges in Russia released amid talks between Moscow, Washington
item.description: A U.S. citizen arrested in Russia earlier this month on drug smuggling charges was released ahead of talks in Saudi Arabia between Moscow and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delta plane crash: What we know about CRJ-900, aircraft that flipped over in Toronto
item.description: The CRJ-900 can seat either 70 or 76 people, but the layout and basic dimensions for the craft are nearly identical no matter the capacity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, NewsNation
